sources.notes: For each query X, minimally increment array elements so their bitwise AND is exactly X.
Bilingual solution derives per-element up_X, then accelerates high-to-low bit processing with AND FWT and inclusion-exclusion.

annotation.summary: For many targets X, find the minimum total increments so all adjusted numbers AND to exactly X.
annotation.constraints: n,q up to 2e5 and values below 2^20; an O(nq) per-query scan is impossible.
annotation.core_idea: First every adjusted value must satisfy y & X = X. For a single x, the cheapest up_X(x) is obtained by changing the highest bit where x violates X and setting lower bits to fit X. After all elements are lifted this way, their AND Y may still contain extra 1 bits. The highest extra bit can be fixed by one more carefully chosen element, avoiding the case where all other elements keep that bit. To batch queries, process bits of X high-to-low and aggregate costs for masks containing required prefix bits but not the next bit using AND FWT plus inclusion-exclusion.
annotation.complexity: O(B 2^B + q B) for B=20, plus O(n B) preprocessing.
tricks: minimum lift to a supermask of X
fix highest extra AND bit with one element

solution_variants.name: and-fwt-batched-greedy
solution_variants.summary: Batch all queries by mask using AND FWT/inclusion-exclusion, then apply the greedy one-element correction for extra AND bits.

solution_variants.name: direct-per-query-greedy
solution_variants.summary: Compute up_X(a_i) for every element and then fix extra bits directly; useful for understanding but too slow for constraints.

tags.evidence: The optimized solution uses AND Fast Walsh-Hadamard Transform to aggregate costs over masks containing required bits.
tags.description: Fast Walsh-Hadamard Transform specialized for subset/superset convolution over bitwise AND masks.
tags.created_reason: AND-FWT is a concrete FWT subtype useful for high-rating bitmask aggregation queries.

tags.evidence: The batched cost computation uses inclusion-exclusion over required and forbidden bit conditions.
All feasibility and cost aggregation is over 20-bit masks and bitwise AND constraints.
The per-element lift and final correction both choose the highest problematic bit greedily.
